title: Bitcoin ETFs shed record $6.4B in 30 days amid crypto winter chill
summary: US-listed spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds experienced their largest 30-day net outflow since launching in 2024, totaling $6.4 billion. This coincides with Bitcoin's 17% price drop over the past month, reflecting broader market uncertainty.
body:
US-listed spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have recorded their biggest 30-day net outflow since their launch in 2024, shedding a total of $6.4 billion. This significant withdrawal comes as Bitcoin's price has fallen by 17% over the past month, highlighting the ongoing challenges in the crypto market.

The outflows mark a stark contrast to the initial enthusiasm surrounding Bitcoin ETFs, which saw massive inflows when they first launched. The recent sell-off suggests that investors are becoming more cautious amid a broader crypto winter, characterized by declining prices and reduced market confidence.

For everyday investors, this trend indicates a shift in market sentiment and potential volatility. Those holding Bitcoin ETFs may experience further price fluctuations, and new investors should be aware of the risks associated with market downturns. It's a reminder to diversify portfolios and stay informed about market trends.

Looking ahead, investors should monitor Bitcoin's price movements and any regulatory developments that could impact the crypto market. Keep an eye on the next Federal Reserve meeting, as interest rate decisions could influence investor sentiment and market behavior.
twitterThread: Bitcoin ETFs just saw their biggest 30-day outflow ever: $6.4B. Bitcoin's price dropped 17% in the same period. What's going on?
The record outflow comes as the crypto market faces a 'winter' of declining prices and investor caution. Bitcoin ETFs, once a hot investment, are now seeing massive withdrawals.

If you're holding Bitcoin ETFs, expect more volatility. Watch the next Fed meeting for clues on market direction. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-21-bitcoin-etfs-see-record-64b-outflow-as-crypto-winter-deepens
